calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
MINI MINUTES: Courtney Rockwell-Gehrett, Esq. appearing for Tirzah Anne Lassahn, Trustee.
Katelyn Phillips, Esq. appearing representing the San Francisco Public Guardian, Conservator of the Person.

The following matters were called together:

1) PCN-24-307001, Conservatorship of Robert P. Clarke; and
2) PTR-24-307568, Robert P. Clarke Revocable Trust

The Public Guardian has no objection to the following:

A. First Account and Report of Conservator and Petition for (1) Settlement of Account; (2) Ratification of Trustee's Acts; (3) Increase of Conservator's Bond; (4) Award of Conservator's Fees; (5) Award of Attorneys' Fees; and (6) Set Date of Next Compliance hearing
B. First Account and Report of Trustee and Petition for (1) Settlement of Account; (2) Ratification of Trustee's Acts; (3) Decrease of Trustee's Bond; (4) Award of Trustee's Fees; and (5) Award of Attorneys' Fees

Court hereby reduced fees by $2,000.00.
Taken under submission with the intent to grant with modification. Court to issue order.

Judge: Michael McNaughton; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Instructions Regarding Authority Of Trustee And Conservator To Sell Conservatee?S Real Property And Authority To Enter Exclusive Listing Agreement With Real Estate Agent
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Gruber appeared via zoom for Tirzah Lassahn (petitioner).
Judy Hitchcock appeared via zoom for Robert Clarke (Court Appointed Counsel).
Benjamin Bernard appeared via zoom for SFPG.

Discussion was held regarding the sale of the property, that the proceeds of the sale would go to pay for the conservatee's care, and that there is no objections to the sale. The matter is taken under submission with the intent to allow the sale.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Bloom. Not reported.

Courtney Rockwell-Gehrett appeared for trustee Tirzah Anne Lassahn (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Instructions Regarding Trustee and Conservator Authority to Encumber Real Property, and Grant of Independent Authority to Lease Conservatee's Real Property.

Counsel advised that petitioner intended to withdraw the instant petition and file an amended petition seeking authority to sell the real property. Based on counsel's representations, the Court ordered the matter off calendar.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing Of The First Account
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Continued To Nov-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called together:
1) PCN-24-307001, Conservatorship of Robert P. Clarke; and
2) PTR-24-307568, Robert P. Clarke Revocable Trust.

Appearances:
Courtney Rockwell-Gehrett for trustee Tirzah Anne Lassahn (via Zoom).
Judy L. Hitchcock, court-appointed attorney for conservatee Robert P. Clarke (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Status of Trustee's Filing First Account; 2) Petition for Instructions; 3) Status on Conservator's Filing First Account.

The court noted that the probate examiner had raised issues regarding the need for updated or corrected inventories and appraisals and inquired as to their status.

Mr. Rockwell-Gehrett reported that corrected inventories and appraisals were being prepared to address discrepancies identified in the initial filings. The corrected versions would include Mr. Clarke's pension and be filed within one week. Mr. Rockwell-Gehrett requested a 45 to 60-day continuance to allow time for filing and service.

The Court continued the matter to November 26, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Dec-30-2024 Continued To Jan-29-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called, and there were no appearances. The court noted that the hearing on the status of filing an inventory and appraisal, which was ordered to be set in a prior court order, may have been overlooked. The court opted to continue the hearing on status of filing an inventory and appraisal to January 29, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
